patch.value: Implement a first-class pm mutation for redacting sensitive strings from append-only history streams without hand-editing JSONL files, preserving replay integrity by recomputing affected history hashes or recording a verifiable redaction marker.

A supported pm command can redact configured literals/patterns from item files and history streams; history drift validation remains meaningful after redaction; redaction operations are themselves auditable; secret scans can include history streams without false failures from known private operations paths.

value.author: codex-audit
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm search history stream redaction command redact private path history drift --limit 10 found only closed history/security items; current audit discovered remaining private remote paths in historical JSONL streams after current item docs were sanitized.

author: codex-release-agent

value.text: 2026-05-09 duplicate-check evidence: pm search tracker history privacy redaction private paths --limit 10 found this existing item as the canonical match. Current scan found no raw IP/password/token values in non-history tracked files; current pm item files only mention Sentry token variable names, but historical JSONL streams still include private remote paths from older telemetry operations. Do not hand-edit history; implement audited redaction support or migrate history safely.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Evidence 2026-05-09: privacy scan confirmed current tracked source/docs/workflows and current item files do not contain raw host/IP/password/token values from this request, but older append-only history streams still contain private infrastructure paths. This remains open because current pm policy forbids direct history rewrites; next work must add an audited redaction command or equivalent safe migration.
